text.title: product-launch Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-launch as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Sliding the launch date because "we need more time to polish" or "one more feature."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each slip breaks partner commitments (hunters, press, amplification partners) and erodes team urgency. The polish is rarely meaningful; the reputational cost is real.

failure-mode.description: Creating a vague checklist like "marketing assets" or "everything ready" without owner names or done criteria.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vague checklists create diffusion of responsibility. On launch day, critical pieces are missing because no one thought they owned them.

failure-mode.description: LLMs or overeager teams announcing "we expect 100K signups, top 3 on Product Hunt" without grounding in comparable launches.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unrealistic targets train the team to ignore actual performance and repeat failures. The public announcement creates reputational pressure to spin results.

failure-mode.description: Automating all social posts on launch day, then watching a typo or broken link go live to 50K followers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bots miss visual issues and tone problems. A human review gate (60 min) catches these before publish.

failure-mode.description: Skipping the postmortem because "the launch is over, on to the next thing."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without reflection, the team repeats the same mistakes on every launch. Each launch becomes chaos instead of a repeatable playbook that gets incrementally better.
